agent_memory.purpose: Nuanced concepts for a portfolio construction/stock picking agent (not a trading agent).

sections.topic: U.S. Regulation Drive-Train (Menthol, Nicotine Caps, PMTAs, SE Pathway)
sections.context: Federal actions (menthol cigarette rule, potential nicotine-reduction standards) and FDA product authorizations (PMTAs) are the primary drivers of what may legally remain on shelves. Substantial Equivalence (SE) still permits certain combustibles reformulations tied to 2007 predicates, but timelines can affect SKU refresh and packaging changes.
sections.investment_insight: Underwrite U.S. EBIT by overlaying (1) menthol mix and state-level bans, (2) nicotine-cap scenarios and compliance clocks, and (3) PMTA status by platform/SKU. Favor portfolios with already-authorized reduced-risk products and diversified earnings beyond U.S. combustibles.
metrics_raw: Menthol federal rule: withdrawn/delayed in early 2025; state/local actions persist
Nicotine cap: proposal stage in 2025; comments due Sep 2025; potential ~2-year compliance post-finalization
PMTA wins: oral nicotine pouches (e.g., ZYN) authorized Jan 2025; JUUL device + tobacco/menthol pods authorized Jul 2025
SE predicate date: Feb 15, 2007; reformulations may trigger SE/PMTA reviews

sections.topic: State/Local Price Architecture (Coupon Bans, Floor Prices, Flavor Bans)
sections.context: Beyond excise taxes, municipalities increasingly ban coupons/discounting and set minimum retail prices (some cities ~$15/pack), compressing promotional levers and raising realized net price. These rules interact with excise/settlement pass-through and can push demand to bordering areas or illicit channels.
sections.investment_insight: Model realized price/mix at the city/county level. In coupon-ban or price-floor markets, assume higher gross realization but also elevated cross-border leakage and elasticity among price-sensitive cohorts.
metrics_raw: Jurisdictions with coupon bans/minimum prices (track quarterly)
Border-county sales divergence vs. state averages post-policy
Promo spend as % of gross to sustain share under discount bans

sections.topic: Scanner-Data Blind Spots (Specialty Vape & Online Channels)
sections.context: NielsenIQ/IRI largely miss vape specialty stores and many online channels, understating ENDS category size and shifting shares. Shipments and PMTA authorization lists better reflect the legally marketable base.
sections.investment_insight: Do not over-penalize ENDS momentum based on tracked-channel data alone. Triangulate with company shipments, customs imports, retail panels for specialty vape, and the FDA authorized-products list.
metrics_raw: Tracked-channel share vs. shipment-based share deltas
Mix of sales by channel: C-store vs. vape specialty vs. online
Share of revenue tied to authorized SKUs

sections.context: Illicit consumption is material in many markets (often ~10%+). Statewide flavor/menthol bans (e.g., MA, CA) have historically driven cross-border purchasing. EU Track & Trace limits leakage but does not eliminate it.
sections.investment_insight: Use tax-stamp data, border-county divergences, and seizure statistics to adjust reported volume trends. Issuers with strong compliance programs and track-and-trace capabilities are better positioned.
metrics_raw: Illicit share estimates by market (annual)
Border-county pack sales vs. in-state post-ban
Seizure volumes and trend direction

sections.topic: Category Migration Economics (HTP & Oral Pouches vs. Cigarettes/ENDS)
sections.context: Heated tobacco (HTP) and oral nicotine pouches typically carry attractive unit economics and, in some jurisdictions, favorable taxation vs. combustibles. Japan demonstrates HTP-led cigarette displacement with resilient profit pools. In the U.S., IQOS rights returned to PM in 2024 and ILUMA clearance is expected pending FDA decisions; oral pouches gained clarity with 2025 authorizations.
sections.investment_insight: Favor portfolios where reduced-risk products (RRPs) are already through key regulatory gates and where capex/supply chains can scale. Stress-test tax convergence risk that could narrow RRP margin advantage.
metrics_raw: HTP share in Japan (~majority of heated segment; strong IQOS share)
U.S. IQOS rights: PM since Apr 30, 2024; ILUMA marketing order expected 2H25 (company expectations/pending)
Oral nicotine pouches: key SKUs authorized in 2025; watch state excise expansion in 2026+
RRP gross margin vs. combustibles, after excise

sections.context: Master Settlement Agreement (MSA) payments are perpetual, CPI-indexed, and volume-adjusted, creating a structural cost for U.S.-exposed combustibles. Non-Participating Manufacturer (NPM) adjustments and state enforcement disputes can swing annual payments.
sections.investment_insight: In U.S.-heavy names, embed a per-pack MSA burden and scenario-test enforcement/escrow adjustments. Global peers with lower U.S. exposure often merit higher structural margin scores.
metrics_raw: MSA cost per pack (modeled) and YoY indexation
NPM adjustment disputes by state (status, magnitude)
Litigation reserve releases/charges

sections.context: Menthol accounts for roughly one-third of U.S. cigarette consumption. Brand concentration (e.g., Newport, Marlboro Menthol) creates disproportionate downside for menthol-heavy portfolios if federal action resumes or more states adopt bans.
sections.investment_insight: Score issuers by menthol mix and by geographic exposure to ban-prone states. Identify substitution paths (pouches, HTP) and promo budgets needed to retain share post-restriction.
metrics_raw: Menthol mix (% of U.S. volume) by brand/company
State-level flavor/menthol policy map and effective dates
Post-ban category migration (pouches/HTP/illicit) in affected ZIPs

sections.context: Adult demand for combustibles in developed markets is inelastic on average (commonly ~−0.3 to −0.5), allowing price/mix to offset mid-single-digit volume declines. Elasticity is higher for lower-income cohorts and in alternative categories (ENDS/HTP) where switching is easier.
sections.investment_insight: Model net price realization after excise + MSA + local floors/discount bans. Use higher elasticity assumptions in coupon-ban jurisdictions and for non-combustibles where switching costs are lower.
metrics_raw: Elasticity bands by category: combustibles vs. HTP vs. ENDS vs. pouches
Price/mix contribution needed to offset forecast volume decline
Downtrading within brand families vs. private label

sections.context: A unified lens to compare issuers on regulatory risk, mix quality, pricing durability, and data reliability.
sections.investment_insight: Construct a weighted scorecard to rank longs/shorts, then tie to valuation (EV/EBIT, EV/FCF, FCF yield) and capital returns (buybacks/dividends) to find mispriced risk.
metrics_raw: U.S. EBIT at risk: menthol mix, nicotine-cap exposure
PMTA/SE footprint: % of revenue from authorized SKUs
RRP mix & margins: HTP/pouches share and unit economics
Pricing power durability: floors/coupon bans vs. elasticity
MSA burden per pack and litigation flags
Measurement quality: tracked vs. untracked channel reliance
